dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a reported cardiac arrest at 9th and Broad in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What occurred to cause this 911 call?
He was choking on food. Staff performed abdominal thrusts, and then he started coughing.
Mr. Smith, can you state your name for us to confirm you are alert?
Your eyes are open, Mr. Smith. Can you follow our commands?
Could you tell us your age and the date today?
If you can, please raise both of your hands for me.
Mr. Smith, your airway is partially blocked. We'll help clear it now.
We're going to suction your airway now to remove the food.
Mr. Smith's breathing is 21 per minute, and we'll monitor for changes.
Oxygen saturation is at 84% with room air.
Pulse is 83, stable with a paced rhythm from your pacemaker.
Blood pressure is 164 over 109. You have a history of hypertension.
Skin is pink, warm, and dry. Mr. Smith, you have movement in all extremities.
When did the food first get lodged into your airway?
Mr. Smith is 68 years old and in a wheelchair at the scene.
Are you aware of any allergies to medications or foods?
What medications have you taken recently, Mr. Smith?
When was the last time you had food or drink?
How did you end up choking? Can you tell us what happened?
Are you experiencing chest or throat discomfort right now?
Does any movement or breathing affect that discomfort?
Blood glucose is currently unable to be completed.
The EKG shows a paced rhythm, matching your pacemaker history.
Mr. Smith, we'll now start an IV in your arm for treatment needs.
Respiratory rate is 21 and showing typical breathing patterns.
Oxygen level is 84%. We'll check it again later.
Heart rate is 83 and beating with a normal paced rhythm.
Blood pressure is 164 over 109-minimal change since we first arrived.
IV successfully placed in your extremity vein, Mr. Smith.
Blood glucose reading is 0; unable to complete further measures.
EKG leads are applied for four-lead monitoring under protocol 3-16.
Respiratory rate has increased to 28 since arrival, still within normal breathing.
SpO₂ reading now shows 88%, possibly due to the suctioning we performed earlier.
Heart rate is now 74 with a paced rhythm-slightly down from the initial 83.
Blood pressure remains stable at 152 over 109.
Mr. Smith, did you have chest, head, neck, or abdominal pain before this event?
Mr. Smith says he is not currently experiencing any of those pains.
Mr. Smith, you have a medical history of diabetes-non-insulin dependent. Have you checked your blood sugar lately?
We are following protocol 3-16 for airway obstruction. The food has been suctioned.
Where is the transport destination for Mr. Smith, staff?
Chippenham Hospital is the destination.
Mr. Smith, we'll assist you to the stretcher now. Are you ready?
Mr. Smith is now secured with seatbelts and railings.
Tailgate is down; we are en route to Chippenham Hospital.
Mr. Smith, if you feel difficulty breathing, please tell us immediately.
Estimated time to the hospital is 15 minutes.
Vital signs: Pulse 74, respirations 28, BP 152/109, SpO2 88%, glucose 0, and EKG paced.
The airway is clear. Breathing is still regular but slightly increased.
Mr. Smith is doing well considering the circumstances up to now.
If you need assistance with breathing at any time, just let us know.
Mr. Smith is stable and shows no new symptoms during transport.
Thank you for cooperating; it allows us to give you better care.
The emergency team at Chippenham has been notified and is prepared.
The hospital has been updated on Mr. Smith's condition.
Mr. Smith is AOX4-alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event.
Mr. Smith has seizure, TBI, and schizoid personality history, but nothing is active now.
Mr. Smith, please stay in your current position and continue breathing as is.
We are preparing to assist you into the emergency department bed.
You'll be assessed by the emergency team as soon as we arrive.
Please keep calm; your care is ongoing and well-coordinated.
Mr. Smith is AOX4-fully oriented in person, place, time, and event.
Mr. Smith is not currently reporting any pain.
IV line established in the extremity vein without complications.
